change.title: AI-assisted refund approval and ERP sync
change.summary: Adds an AI agent that recommends refund approvals, updates billing roles, stores customer refund notes, and syncs invoice status to the ERP webhook.
change.diff_excerpt: agent can call approve_refund tool; role check moved client-side; webhook retry accepts unsigned events; customer email included in audit payload
change.business_context: Finance operations team wants AI-speed refund handling without exposing customer data or approving fraudulent refunds.

risk_signals.impact: Payment regressions can create fraud, duplicate refunds, or revenue loss.
AI agent changes can create unsafe tool calls or unreviewed decisions.
Integration failures can silently corrupt downstream systems.
Unauthorized access or privilege escalation can reach production users.
Sensitive data exposure can create privacy and compliance incidents.
State-machine bugs can approve, skip, or duplicate business workflows.

release_gates.title: Verify refund approvals require server-side policy and the correct approver.
release_gates.expected_evidence: passing regression evidence plus signed audit event

release_gates.title: Verify retry and failure paths cannot duplicate invoice or refund records.
release_gates.expected_evidence: passing regression evidence plus signed audit event

release_gates.title: Verify prompt injection cannot trigger an irreversible tool call.
release_gates.expected_evidence: passing regression evidence plus signed audit event

release_gates.title: Verify the agent requires human approval before release-blocking or financial actions.
release_gates.expected_evidence: passing regression evidence plus signed audit event

release_gates.title: Verify webhook signatures and trusted-source checks are enforced.
release_gates.expected_evidence: passing regression evidence plus signed audit event

release_gates.title: Verify replay, timeout, and partial-failure handling remains idempotent.
release_gates.expected_evidence: passing regression evidence plus signed audit event

release_gates.expected_evidence: passing regression evidence plus signed audit event

release_gates.title: Verify audit payloads contain only fields allowed for the user's role.

audit_events.details: AI-assisted refund approval and ERP sync

recommended_plan.name: Growth
recommended_plan.target_customer: SaaS teams shipping weekly with AI coding tools

metering.gross_margin_story: Deterministic scan cost stays near zero; optional LLM review is metered per release.

judge_summary.market_friction: AI coding accelerates releases, but governance, test coverage, and audit evidence lag behind.
judge_summary.target_cohort: SaaS engineering teams using AI coding agents for payments, auth, data, or workflow changes.
judge_summary.monetization: Monthly SaaS tiers plus usage-based release scan overages.
judge_summary.why_now: Every AI-generated pull request needs a release guardian before production.
